en.extract:
The red-crowned malimbe (Malimbus coronatus) is a species of bird in the family Ploceidae (members of which are called weavers).
It is found in across central Africa, in Cameroon, Central African Republic, Republic of the Congo, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Equatorial Guinea, and Gabon. It is and mostly found in the forest canopy. Due to its large range, it is classified as least concern, though the population is thought to be declining.
Adult females of the species are all-black, while males have red on the crown.

The '''red-crowned malimbe''' ('''''Malimbus coronatus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Ploceidae]] (members of which are called weavers).
It is found in across central Africa, in [[Cameroon]], [[Central African Republic]], [[Republic of the Congo]], [[Democratic Republic of the Congo]], [[Equatorial Guinea]], and [[Gabon]]. It is and mostly found in the forest [[Canopy (botany)|canopy]].<ref name="ebird">{{cite web |url=https://ebird.org/species/recmal2 |title=Red-crowned Malimbe |publisher=[[eBird]] |access-date=1 December 2025}}</ref> Due to its large range, it is classified as [[least concern]], though the population is thought to be declining.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://datazone.birdlife.org/species/factsheet/red-crowned-malimbe-malimbus-coronatus |title=Red-crowned Malimbe |publisher=[[BirdLife International]] |access-date=1 December 2025}}</ref>

Adult females of the species are all-black, while males have red on the [[Crown (anatomy)|crown]].<ref name="ebird"/>
